steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence states a general principle: sleek vehicles are more aerodynamic. The second sentence introduces the T-45 Goshawk jet as a case that illustrates that principle. The blank must signal the move from a general claim to a specific example.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — meanwhile,: signals something happening at the same time as something else. There is no simultaneous event here — the jet is an example of the general principle, not a parallel event. ✗
  • Choice B — however,: signals contrast or contradiction. The jet’s aerodynamics do not contradict the principle — they confirm it. ✗
  • Choice C — for instance,: signals that what follows is a specific example of what was just stated. The T-45 Goshawk is exactly that — a specific sleek vehicle that is aerodynamic. ✓
  • Choice D — additionally,: signals an additional, separate point. The jet is not a new point; it is an illustration of the same point. ✗
That matches Choice C — for instance,.
